disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden unheralded syncope while seated at rest, with no prodrome (no nausea, warmth or tunnel vision)
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when you collapsed?
Did you have any warning before you blacked out - feeling sick, hot, or dizzy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was just sitting at the table, no warning at all - my heart fluttered hard and then I woke up on the floor.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A brief, intense palpitation (heart flutter) immediately preceded the loss of consciousness
trigger_question_examples: Did you feel anything in your chest just before you blacked out?
Was your heart doing anything unusual right beforehand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes - my heart fluttered really violently for a second, and then everything went black.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Family history of sudden cardiac death at a young age - a brother had a cardiac arrest at 28
trigger_question_examples: Has anyone in your family died suddenly or young, or had a cardiac arrest?
Any family history of heart rhythm problems or unexplained sudden death?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes - my brother had a sudden cardiac arrest at 28. That's why I'm so scared.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: No, no family history I can think of.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Early-onset atrial fibrillation in a young relative - mother diagnosed with atrial fibrillation in her 30s
trigger_question_examples: Any family history of an irregular or fast heartbeat, like atrial fibrillation?
Have any young relatives had heart rhythm problems?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My mum was diagnosed with atrial fibrillation in her 30s.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Frequent unprovoked palpitations or awareness of a rapid heartbeat over the last six months
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any episodes of your heart racing or fluttering before this?
Any palpitations over the last few months?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, I've had lots of unprovoked flutters and a racing heart over the last six months, but I just ignored them.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain, and no exertional trigger (occurred at rest)
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain before or during the episode?
Were you exerting yourself or exercising at the time?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain at all, and I wasn't doing anything - just sitting down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Not taking any regular or QT-affecting medications and no illicit drug use
trigger_question_examples: Are you on any regular medications?
Any recreational drugs, or recent antibiotics or antidepressants?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I don't take anything at all, and no drugs.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Was it just a normal faint? I feel completely fine now.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Must not accept a simple faint. Should explain that collapsing suddenly at rest with no warning, preceded by a violent palpitation, together with a brother's cardiac arrest at 28, is a red-flag pattern for an inherited heart-rhythm condition; feeling fine now does not remove the risk of a dangerous recurrence, and an urgent heart tracing and monitoring are needed.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Do I really need an ambulance? Could I just see my GP tomorrow?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should explain that this needs urgent hospital assessment now (12-lead ECG, continuous monitoring) rather than a next-day GP visit, that the risk of a dangerous recurrent rhythm is high given the family history, and that an ambulance is appropriate while remaining still and calm.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient may downplay it as a one-off faint and want to defer - tests whether the AI Doctor recognises a probable inherited channelopathy and secures urgent ECG/monitoring rather than reassuring.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: I feel totally fine now, it was probably just a one-off faint.
